name: Thread-safe LRU cache
task: Create /tmp/eval_tsl/tsl.py with class ThreadSafeLRU(capacity). Standard LRU get/put semantics: get(key) returns the value or -1 on miss and marks the key most-recently-used; put(key, value) inserts/updates, marks it most-recently-used, and when size exceeds capacity evicts the least-recently-used key. Every public operation MUST acquire a threading.Lock (or RLock) constructed in __init__, so the cache is safe under concurrent use from multiple threads.

validation.script:
import importlib.util, threading, re, sys
src = open('/tmp/eval_tsl/tsl.py').read()
# strip block/# comments and string literals for the source-level lock check
_s = re.sub(r'#.*', '', src)
_s = re.sub(r'"""(?:.|\n)*?"""', ' ', _s)
_s = re.sub(r"'''(?:.|\n)*?'''", ' ', _s)
_s = re.sub(r'"(?:\\.|[^"\\])*"', ' ', _s)
_s = re.sub(r"'(?:\\.|[^'\\])*'", ' ', _s)
assert re.search(r'threading\.(?:Lock|RLock)\s*\(', _s), 'must construct a threading.Lock/RLock'
assert re.search(r'\bwith\s+self\.\w+\b', _s) or re.search(r'\.acquire\s*\(', _s), 'must acquire the lock around operations'
spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_tsl/tsl.py')
mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
c = mod.ThreadSafeLRU(2)
c.put(1,'a'); c.put(2,'b'); assert c.get(1)=='a'; c.put(3,'c'); assert c.get(2)==-1
# concurrent distinct-key eviction stress: each thread inserts its own disjoint key range
cap=8; NT=8; PER=1500
cc = mod.ThreadSafeLRU(cap); errs=[]
def worker(tid):
    try:
        base=tid*PER
        for i in range(PER):
            cc.put(base+i, tid)
            cc.get(base + (i//2))
    except Exception as e:
        errs.append(repr(e))
ts=[threading.Thread(target=worker,args=(t,)) for t in range(NT)]
for t in ts: t.start()
for t in ts: t.join()
assert not errs, 'concurrent access raised: %r' % errs[:3]
# functional integrity after stress: a fresh sequential check still works
c2 = mod.ThreadSafeLRU(2); c2.put(1,'a'); c2.put(2,'b'); c2.put(3,'c')
assert c2.get(1)==-1 and c2.get(2)=='b' and c2.get(3)=='c'
print('OK')
